brief_justification: Paper A is a geometric/topological-field-theory study of 3d mirror symmetry between hyperkahler intermediate Jacobians of a Calabi-Yau 3-fold, using SYZ transformations, derived categories with varying stability conditions, and Rozansky-Witten A/B-branes; paper B is a numerical-linear-algebra contraction algorithm (SI-CTMRG) that replaces large truncated SVDs with QR-based projectors and small SVDs to accelerate iPEPS on GPUs. No role-for-role correspondence exists: A's mechanism is a geometric duality of moduli spaces while B's is an eigen/singular-subspace iteration for matrix truncation, so the only overlap is generic linear-algebra vocabulary, not shared machinery.
